diagrams.title: Vector Space V and Subspace W
diagrams.description: Shows a vector space V (like R³) with a subspace W (like a plane through origin) inside it
elements: Outer oval represents entire vector space V
Inner shaded plane represents subspace W passing through origin (0,0,0)
Origin point clearly shown at intersection
Arrows showing vectors in W are all within the plane
diagrams.purpose: Visualize subspace as a subset that contains the origin and is closed under operations

diagrams.title: Linear Combination and Span
diagrams.description: Two vectors in R³ and their span forming a plane through origin
elements: Vector v₁ shown in one direction
Vector v₂ shown in different direction
Origin at intersection of both vectors
diagrams.purpose: Understand span as all linear combinations forming a subspace

diagrams.title: Basis Vectors in R²
diagrams.description: Standard basis vectors î and ĵ in the xy-plane
elements: Any vector (a,b) shown as combination aî + bĵ
diagrams.purpose: Understand basis as minimal set that spans the entire space

diagrams.title: Linearly Dependent vs Independent
diagrams.description: Comparison of dependent (collinear) and independent vectors in R²
elements: Left: Two vectors on same line - dependent
Right: Two vectors at angle - independent
Shows that dependent vectors provide redundant information
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish between linear dependence and independence visually
